---
title: "Theoretical and Empirical Research Methodology"
subtitle: "Lecture and implementation lab in the fall semester 2026"
format: html
---

<!---
::: {.callout-important collapse=false}
## 📢 Important

(DD/MM/2026)

- ...

:::

--->

# 📍 When and where

- **Main lecture:** Mondays
- **Implementation lab:** Fridays

The lecture period runs from **14 September to 11 December 2026**. There is no
class in the week of 12–16 October. Please always check the most recent version
of the seminar timetable, since dates and rooms can differ from one week to
another. The authoritative timetable, rooms and times are [here](/content/material/index.qmd).
For all kinds of official announcement please watch the
[Moodle course](https://elearning.uni-flensburg.de/moodle/course/view.php?id=17920).

You find the full schedule on the [lecture overview](/content/lecture/index.qmd) and the
[lab overview](/content/material/index.qmd) pages.

# 💻 Exercises

There is an R package with interactive exercises for the implementation lab.
I strongly recommend to do the exercises for each session since regular
practice is the most important determinant in your success in learning R.
You find more information on how to use the exercises in
[this tutorial](/content/tutorials/using-exercises/index.qmd).
On top of that, there are some additional exercises on selected topics
[here](/content/exercises.qmd).

# 📂 Material

You find an overview of all lecture materials, such as slides and
reading lists, on the [lecture overview](/content/lecture/index.qmd) page, and all lab
materials on the [lab overview](/content/material/index.qmd) page.
Complete material lists for the single sessions are distributed via the
respective session pages. Scientific publications that are not available open
access can be obtained via Moodle.

# 📖 Tutorials

Complementary to the lab sessions there are also short tutorials,
which explain certain concepts in a more detailed and applied manner.
You can find an overview over all tutorials [here](/content/tutorials.qmd).

# 💌 Contact and discussion

For asking questions and starting discussions, please use the forum in 
[Moodle course](https://elearning.uni-flensburg.de/moodle/course/view.php?id=17920).
